text: Extended collaborative writing and architecture session developing the "living workspace" concept for an Orchestrator Studios article series (site deep-dive plus LinkedIn teaser), grounded in the orchestra repo and Orchestrator Studios website. The conversation worked through core ideas â€” applications as user-facing exes over a collapsed application/data "substrate," AI-in-the-loop chatbots on the substrate, and the "let there be light" magic moments of building live on-site â€” and distilled them into a manifesto ("The Repo Is the Application"), a concepts catalog doc, and a README anchored on a concrete definition starting from an empty folder structure. It then restructured the living-workspace repo into three cleanly separated parts (canon of how the system works, a worked example, and a fresh starter template), set up git with the correct Orchestra Studios GitHub org remote origin, and rejected using commits-as-history in favor of an in-repo visible timeline. Much of the later work centered on getting journey.md right â€” reframing the example as a structured literature-review research project told in "hops," prioritizing crystal-clear plain writing over style, and accounting for steps that create schemas and tools â€” ending mid-effort on a full journey.md redo that starts by pinning down the final deliverable and where it belongs in the repo.
